title: Turn a Content Brief into a Valid Tree
summary: Elements create relationships through start tags, end tags, and nesting. Indentation makes those relationships readable to people but does not create them. A child must close before its parent closes, while void elements such as img and meta do not receive end tags. Predict, inspect, explain, and apply the contract in a cumulative public-information build.
objectives: Explain html elements nesting with source and browser evidence.
Apply html elements nesting without discarding earlier document work.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed public-service scenario.
